'''Jacques O'Neill''' (born 8 May 1999) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hk}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan and dual registration at the [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]], and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

In 2022, he was a contestant on the [[Love Island (2015 TV series, series 8)|eighth series]] of ''[[Love Island (2015 TV series)|Love Island]]''.

==Early life==
O'Neill was born in [[Barrow-in-Furness]], [[Cumbria, England]].

He played junior rugby league for [[Askam ARLFC]] before being signed to the [[Castleford Tigers]] academy. After earning his academy contract, he was driven from Cumbria to Castleford twice a week for training by his mother.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=10 January 2021 |title=Cas star Jacques O'Neill aiming high in 2021 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/cas-star-jacques-oneill-aiming-high-in-2021/ |access-date=11 June 2022 |website=TotalRL}}</ref><ref name="Cas Biography" /> He currently lives in [[Leeds]].

==Club career==
=== Castleford Tigers ===
In August 2015, at the age of 16, O'Neill signed his first professional contract with the [[Castleford Tigers]] after progressing through the club's academy.<ref name="Pro contract">{{Cite web |date=2015-08-07 |title=O'Neill inks pro contract at Castleford |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/oneill-inks-pro-contract-at-castleford/ |access-date=2022-07-08 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> The contract spanned two-and-a-half years and would see him step up into the Tigers' under-19s.<ref name="Pro contract" />

==== Leigh Centurions (loan) ====
In 2018, O'Neill joined the [[Leigh Centurions]] on a short-term loan to play in the [[Championship Shield]] Final.<ref>{{Cite web |date=7 October 2018 |title=Featherstone 42 Leigh 10: Newman shines as Rovers lift Championship Shield |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-42-leigh-10-newman-shines-rovers-lift-championship-shield-245366 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He was drafted in alongside 6 other academy players from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] after the club revealed they would not have enough fit players to fulfil the fixture.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 October 2018 |title=Wigan Warriors loan crisis-hit Leigh FOUR players for final |url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-loan-crisis-hit-leigh-four-players-final-568377 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=Wigan Today}}</ref>

==== Halifax (dual registration) ====
In 2019, O'Neill played one game for [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] through their dual registration arrangement with Castleford.

==== Breakthrough ====
On 1 March 2019, O'Neill made his [[Super League]] début for Castleford against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 March 2019 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 32-16 Hull KR |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47373260 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In August, he signed a new one-year deal with the Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web |date=8 August 2019 |title=Jacques O'Neill: Castleford Tigers hooker signs new one-year extension |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49279507 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first try on 10 August against the [[London Broncos]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 August 2019 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 20-6 London Broncos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49249961 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and finished his breakthrough season with 11 appearances.

O'Neill signed a further one-year contract extension with Castleford in September 2020; head coach [[Daryl Powell]] said of him, "Attitude wise he is spot on. He gets after things. He needs to stay focused and I'm sure he will, and he will end up being an outstanding rugby league player."<ref>{{Cite web |date=6 September 2020 |title=Jacques O'Neill: Castleford Hooker signs one-year contract extension |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54050179 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> O'Neill made 12 appearances and scored 2 tries throughout the 2020 season, and was voted as the [[League Express]] Young Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 January 2021 |title=McShane & O'Neill win in League Express readers' poll |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7216 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=3 January 2021 |title=McShane wins League Express Player of the Year poll |url=https://www.totalrl.com/mcshane-wins-league-express-player-of-the-year-poll/ |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

In June 2021, O'Neill signed a new two-year deal with Castleford, stating, "I've been at Cas since I was 15/16 and it has a place in my heart now, I wasn't really looking to go anywhere else."<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 June 2021 |title=New deal for O'Neill |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7466 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> A persistent [[Pulled hamstring|hamstring injury]] severely disrupted his season, limiting him to just 6 appearances and forcing him to miss the [[2021 Challenge Cup final]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=25 June 2021 |title=Castleford Tigers hooker Jacques O'Neill joins casualty list three weeks ahead of Challenge Cup final meeting with St Helens |work=The Yorkshire Evening Post |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/oneill-is-wembley-concern-for-tigers-3286090 |access-date=8 April 2022}}</ref><ref name="Injury">{{Cite web |date=17 November 2021 |title=O'Neill on journey back to full fitness |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7750 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He underwent a hamstring re-attachment operation after his campaign was cut short, and began a steady rehabilitation process.<ref name="Injury" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=1 December 2021 |title=Jacques talks rehab progression |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7771 |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

On 10 June 2022, Castleford announced that O'Neill had been released from his contract "to allow him to pursue another opportunity" of appearing on the [[ITV (TV network)|ITV]] reality show [[Love Island (2015 TV series, series 8)|''Love Island'']].<ref name="Cas Release">{{Cite web |date=10 June 2022 |title=Club Statement: Jacques O'Neill |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8112 |access-date=11 June 2022 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=11 June 2022 |title=Jacques O'Neill: Castleford Tigers release player to go on Love Island |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61769276 |access-date=11 June 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> The Tigers had the first option to sign O'Neill back if he decided to return to rugby league in the next year.<ref name="Cas Release" /> On 13 July, immediately following O'Neill's exit from the show, he visited a training session at the club, although regarding a return head coach [[Lee Radford]] said, "It is something we've not spoken about. That is probably a conversation to have further down the line I think."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2022-07-13 |title=Jacques O'Neill visits Castleford team-mates after leaving Love Island |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jacques-oneill-castleford-tigers-team-mates-leave-love-island/ |access-date=2022-07-13 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref name="Cas Welfare">{{Cite web |date=2022-07-13 |title=Club Statement: Jacques O'Neill |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8170 |access-date=2022-07-13 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

=== Return to Castleford Tigers ===
On 20 April 2023, it was announced that O'Neill had rejoined the [[Castleford Tigers]] on an initial contract until the end of the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 Apr 2023|accessdate=20 Apr 2023|title=Jacques O'Neill: Love Island star returns to Castleford Tigers until end of season|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65343168|website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=20 April 2023 |title=O'Neill returns to the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8569 |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=20 April 2023 |title=Love Island star Jacques O'Neill makes Super League return with Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/12860689/love-island-star-jacques-oneill-makes-super-league-return-with-castleford-tigers |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> Given his 18-month long absence from the sport and his previous hamstring issue, head physiotherapist [[Matt Crowther]] "worked closely with him to try and get him more mobile" and "durable" again.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=13 June 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal playmaker suffers injury setback as latest on Jacques O'Neill explained |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-reveal-playmaker-suffers-injury-setback-as-latest-on-jacques-oneill-explained/ |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> A sore [[Achilles tendon|Achilles]] meant this took longer than anticipated,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=29 June 2023 |title=Will Jacques O'Neill play for Castleford Tigers in Super League 2023? Andy Last has his say |url=https://www.totalrl.com/will-jacques-oneill-play-for-castleford-tigers-in-super-league-2023-andy-last-has-his-say/ |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref> so a loan move was utilised to build up match fitness.<ref name="LRL Sheffield Loan">{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=15 August 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers hooker Jacques O'Neill makes Championship loan move |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-jacques-oneill-makes-sheffield-eagles-championship-loan-move |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

==== Sheffield Eagles (loan) ====
In August 2023, O'Neill joined the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on an initial two-week loan deal, continuing on a rolling basis.<ref name="LRL Sheffield Loan" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=15 August 2023 |title=O'Neill joins Sheffield on loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8724 |access-date=22 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> However, early into this move, he suffered a further setback with his Achilles injury, and was unable to make an appearance for the Eagles.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=16 August 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers' Jacques O'Neill suffers injury heartbreak as Sheffield Eagles loan move looms |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-jacques-oneill-suffers-injury-heartbreak-as-sheffield-eagles-loan-move-looms/ |access-date=22 October 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

==== Departure ====
In October 2023, following the conclusion of his half-year deal, O'Neill was not included in Castleford's listed squad for the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=22 October 2023 |title=Full 2024 first team squad |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8796 |access-date=22 October 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> New head coach [[Craig Lingard]] later clarified, "Jacques O'Neill is not at the club, his contract hasn't been renewed and he has naturally left the club as people do at the end of their contracts."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=30 October 2023 |title=When Castleford Tigers players will return for pre-season as Craig Lingard gives update on Albert Vete, Jacob Miller and Jacques O'Neill future |url=https://www.totalrl.com/when-castleford-tigers-players-will-return-for-pre-season-as-craig-lingard-gives-update-on-albert-vete-jacob-miller-and-jacques-oneill-future/ |access-date=12 December 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

One year later, reflecting on his previous attempted return to rugby league, O'Neill said, "I was in a really bad place mentally and physically the worst shape of my life. I came back and expected to be the old Jacques who was playing Super League and it was just not the right timing."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=10 October 2024 |title=Jacques O'Neill launches rugby league return with determined stance set out |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jacques-oneill-rugby-league-return-30109768 |access-date=10 October 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 14 November 2024, it was announced that O'Neill had signed for [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=14 November 2024 |title=Jacques O'Neill seals rugby league return with Championship switch revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jacques-oneill-seals-rugby-league-return-with-championship-switch-revealed |access-date=15 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Kennedy |first=Emma |date=14 November 2024 |title=Ex-Love Island star Jacques O'Neill signs for York Knights |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/24725374.ex-love-island-star-jacques-oneill-signs-york-knights/ |access-date=15 November 2024 |website=The York Press}}</ref> In June 2025, O'Neill sustained an ankle injury that ruled him out for the remainder of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/25233263.york-knights-report-major-injury-blow-hooker-jacques-oneill/ |title=York Knights report major injury blow for hooker Jacques O'Neill |website=[[The Press (York)|York Press]] |date=12 June 2025 |access-date=2 June 2026}}</ref> He did not retain his place in the squad for the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/25762043.york-knights-2026-kit-numbers-now-revealed/ |title=York Knights' 2026 kit numbers now revealed |website=[[The Press (York)|York Press]] |date=13 January 2026 |access-date=2 June 2026}}</ref>

== International career ==
O'Neill represented [[England national rugby league team|England Under-16s]] in their international series against [[France national rugby league team|France Under-16s]] in April 2015.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-02-11 |title=England Youth and Academy squads named for 2015 |url=http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/31614/england-youth-and-academy-squads |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150912105606/http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/31614/england-youth-and-academy-squads |archive-date=2015-09-12 |access-date=2022-07-08 |website=The RFL}}</ref> He played as a hooker in both the 16–6 victory at [[Belle Vue (Wakefield)|Belle Vue]] on 3 April, scoring a try and receiving a Man of the Match award,<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=584068255184248832|title=England U16s 16 - France U16s 6 -Congrats to Castleford Tiger Jacques O'Neill with a try scoring debut and MOM #COYF}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Daly |first=Phil |date=2015-04-03 |title=England Youth enjoy Good Friday win |url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2015/04/03/england-youth-enjoy-good-friday-win/ |access-date=2022-07-08 |website=Leeds Rhinos}}</ref> and the 20–8 defeat at [[Post Office Road]] on 7 April.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Daly |first=Phil |date=2015-04-08 |title=France Under-16s too strong for England Youth |url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2015/04/08/france-under-16s-too-strong-for-england-youth/ |access-date=2022-07-08 |website=Leeds Rhinos}}</ref>

In April 2021, O'Neill was selected in the [[England Knights]] performance squad.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thomson |first=Doug |date=27 April 2021 |title=England Knights squad named |url=https://www.totalrl.com/england-knights-squad-named/ |access-date=8 April 2022 |website=TotalRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=27 April 2021 |title=Three Tigers in England Knights squad! |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7378 |access-date=27 April 2021 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

== Personal life ==
He was diagnosed with [[ADHD]] at age 9.<ref>{{cite news |date=9 July 2022 |title=Love Island Jacques' has ADHD and struggles with emotions, says star's family |newspaper=Grimsbylive |url=https://www.grimsbytelegraph.co.uk/news/celebs-tv/love-island-jacques-oneill-diagnosed-7312568}}</ref>

In 2022, O'Neill became a contestant on the [[Love Island (2015 TV series, series 8)|eighth series]] of ''[[Love Island (2015 TV series)|Love Island]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 June 2022 |title=Rugby league star Jacques O'Neill confirmed as new Love Island contestant |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jacques-oneill-love-island-owen-24200394 |website=Yorkshire Live |accessdate=10 June 2022}}</ref> He decided to leave the villa on Day 37 for mental health purposes and to "get back to himself".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Percival |first=Ash |date=2022-07-13 |title=Love Island's Jacques O'Neill Tearfully Explains His Exit From Show |url=https://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/entry/love-island-jacques-oneill-explains-exit_uk_62ce72fbe4b0aa392d4601b4 |access-date=2022-07-13 |website=The Huffington Post}}</ref> [[Castleford Tigers]] offered O'Neill welfare support and spoke of the club's focus to support and welcome him at [[Wheldon Road]].<ref name="Cas Welfare" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-07-13 |title=Jacques O'Neill: Castleford Tigers offer welfare support after 23-year-old's Love Island exit |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12589481/jacques-oneill-castleford-tigers-offer-welfare-support-after-23-year-olds-love-island-exit |access-date=2022-07-13 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>
